content: The intrinsic reward signals used to rank novel states in curiosity-driven RL (ICM/RND vs. curious agent comparisons) can be repurposed as a dynamic memory pruning criteria for SOTA active learning agents with long episodic memory. This creates a self-sustaining curious active learning loop where the agent only retains memory traces that trigger the most intrinsic surprise, rather than pruning based on fixed task loss or recency rules.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
